title: Archive: Proximity / FingerEye Sensing
description: Archive reason: FingerEye completes the proximity / pre-contact-to-contact tactile sensor idea by building a compact vision-tactile fingertip sensor with binocular close-range RGB perception, implicit stereo depth, compliant-ring deformation sensing, and dexterous manipulation policy learning.
summary: The standalone proximity / FingerEye idea is archived. The original dashboard note wanted hand-object distance and pre-contact sensing before tactile contact; FingerEye directly implements that continuous perception stream, smoothly transitioning from pre-contact visual cues to post-contact tactile feedback, and demonstrates manipulation policies across delicate/dexterous tasks.

details.text: FingerEye directly covers the sensor concept: many tactile sensors only respond after contact, while FingerEye integrates binocular RGB cameras for close-range visual perception before contact and marker-based deformation sensing after external force/torque deforms a compliant ring. That is the pre-contact proximity plus contact-tactile transition the original idea was looking for.

details: Moved-out live work: VTLA should keep broader tactile representation and benchmark choices, such as insertion/fine alignment, opaque-bag search, tactile memory, force/contact fields, and Wuji/Franka data schema. It should not keep proximity/FingerEye as an open subproject.

Archive decision: do not open a separate proximity-sensor build unless the lab is committing to a materially different fingertip hardware design or integrating FingerEye-like sensing into a named robot platform with owner, BOM, and benchmark.
risks_decisions: Archived 2026-06-05 after user flagged FingerEye as completing the proximity / Finger Eye direction.
Reason type: paper/project completed the core continuous pre-contact vision to post-contact tactile sensing idea.
Reopen condition: a lab-specific sensor build or FingerEye integration has a clear hardware owner, platform, data schema, and evaluation task not already covered by the paper.
